mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month change in FEDFUNDS raises the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and tightens financing conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered positive 3-month FEDFUNDS changes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward observations or if rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses upward by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, rising labor slack signals deteriorating income and earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward S&P 500 returns after UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months are not below unconditional returns at p < 0.10 or the sign is positive in most triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: If RSAFS continues rising while FEDFUNDS is above 4.0%, resilient nominal consumer spending offsets tight monetary policy and supports near-term revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.0% and RSAFS 3-month change is positive
failure_condition: Reject if cases with FEDFUNDS > 4.0% and positive 3-month RSAFS growth do not deliver higher 20-day forward S&P 500 returns than cases with negative RSAFS growth, using walk-forward p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month change in INDPRO signals improving real production and operating leverage, leading analysts to raise earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO changes have a nonpositive average 60-day forward S&P 500 return advantage versus negative changes across walk-forward folds.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST crosses below 1500, weakening residential construction signals softer durable-goods demand and credit-sensitive activity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if downward crossings of HOUST below 1500 are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations or show no negative excess return relative to baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL momentum accelerates while FEDFUNDS is already above 4.0%, inflation pressure keeps policy restrictive and raises equity risk premia, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month growth exceeds its 12-month growth and FEDFUNDS > 4.0%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of accelerating CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS > 4.0% is not associated with lower 20-day S&P 500 returns than nontriggered periods in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A positive year-over-year change in GDPC1 indicates expanding real aggregate demand and higher expected corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive year-over-year GDPC1 growth fails to produce a positive 60-day forward return spread versus negative or slowing GDPC1 regimes across at least two consecutive walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS rises over 1 month and UNRATE does not rise, labor income growth without visible slack supports consumer demand and risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 1-month change is positive and UNRATE 1-month change <= 0.0pp
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-positive and UNRATE-nonrising condition has a nonpositive average 5-day S&P 500 forward return or fails to beat unconditional returns in walk-forward validation.
